# latexml-oxide kernel dump v3
# Generated by dump_writer, 943 entries
# Format: <table>\t<key>\t<data>
#   <table> = M | V | C | LC | UC | SC | MC | DC
#   <data> depends on <table>:
#     M/E:   E\t<cs>\t<nargs>\t<flags>\t<body>\t<proto>\t<v3_params>
#            v3_params encodes each Parameter structurally so Until:/Match:
#            delimiter tokens round-trip (see docs/DUMP_FORMAT_PERL_ANALYSIS.md).
#     M/PA:  PA\t<target_cs>   (MPA for math primitives)
#     M/T:   T\t<meaning_token>
#     M/R:   R\t<cs>\t<rtype>\t<value>[\t<mathglyph>]
#     M/N:   N                     (None-meaning sentinel)
#     V:     <type>\t<value>       (B,I,S,CH,T,TK,N,F,G,MG,D,MD,VD)
#     C:                  CC\t<u8>   (catcode)
#     DC/LC/UC/SC/MC:     CH\t<u32>  (char-indexed table entries)
#   <flags> = subset of {L, P} (long, protected)
#   All keys and proto are url-encoded (%09 for tab etc.); token body
#   tokens use the form <catcode_int>:<url_encoded_text> joined by commas.
# Section 0: early let-aliases (12) — target in bootstrap
